name: Photon
tagline: Fast Python web crawler for OSINT that extracts URLs, emails, files, and more
seoTitle: Photon - Fast Python OSINT Web Crawler
metaDescription: Photon is a fast Python web crawler for OSINT. It maps a site and extracts URLs, emails, files, secret keys, JavaScript endpoints, and subdomains.
overview: Photon is a Python web crawler built for OSINT (open-source intelligence) work. As it walks a website, it collects useful data such as in-scope and out-of-scope URLs, URLs with parameters, emails and social media accounts, files like PDFs and images, exposed API keys and hashes, JavaScript files and their endpoints, and subdomain/DNS information. The results are saved in an organized folder and can also be exported as JSON.

It is aimed at security researchers, bug bounty hunters, and anyone who needs to map a site and pull structured data out of it. You run it from the command line against a target URL, and it does the crawling and extraction for you.

Within the web scraping and crawling category, Photon focuses on reconnaissance rather than general-purpose page scraping. It uses threaded crawling for speed and offers options to control timeout, delay, seeds, and URL exclusion, plus plugins for archive.org seeds and DNS dumping.
features: Extracts URLs (in-scope and out-of-scope), including URLs with query parameters
Finds intel such as emails, social media accounts, and storage buckets while crawling
Collects files (pdf, png, xml, etc.), secret/API keys, hashes, and JavaScript endpoints
Threaded crawling with options for timeout, delay, seeds, and regex-based URL exclusion
Plugins for wayback (archive.org seeds) and dnsdumpster, plus JSON export and Docker support

gettingStarted.intro: Photon runs as a Python script you clone from GitHub, then point at a target URL from the command line.

steps.heading: Clone the repository
steps.body: Download Photon from GitHub and enter the project directory.

steps.heading: Crawl a target URL
steps.body: Run the script with -u and the site you want to crawl. Extracted data is saved in an organized folder named after the target.

steps.heading: Run with Docker (optional)
steps.body: Build the lightweight Python-Alpine image and run a crawl in a container. Mount a folder to view the results locally.
steps.code:
docker build -t photon .
docker run -it --name photon -v "$PWD:/Photon/google.com" photon:latest -u google.com

useCases: Reconnaissance during a bug bounty or penetration test to map a target site's URLs and endpoints
Finding exposed emails, API keys, or sensitive files left in a site's pages and scripts
Discovering subdomains and DNS data as part of an OSINT investigation
Seeding a crawl from archive.org to find historical URLs that are no longer linked
